description: Compute the standard error of the sample mean — the dispersion of the sampling distribution — using known or estimated population standard deviation divided by the square root of the sample size

fields.name: sample_std_dev
fields.label: s - sample standard deviation (Bessel-corrected, used when sigma unknown)

fields.name: sample_size
fields.label: n - number of observations in the sample

core_formulas.unknown_sigma: SE_hat(X_bar) = s / sqrt(n), where s^2 = sum((X_i - X_bar)^2) / (n - 1)

standard_deviation_vs_error.std_dev: Dispersion of INDIVIDUAL observations around the population mean
standard_deviation_vs_error.std_error: Dispersion of the SAMPLE MEAN around the population mean

distinctions.ratio: SE = sigma / sqrt(n); SE is always smaller than sigma for n > 1

convergence_properties.consistency: As n -> infinity, SE -> 0 and X_bar -> mu

when_to_use_which.known_sigma: Rare in practice; applies when population parameters are pre-specified (e.g., by theoretical model)
when_to_use_which.unknown_sigma: Standard case; use s instead of sigma. For small n, use t-distribution instead of z

applications.confidence_intervals: X_bar +/- z_alpha/2 * SE(X_bar) for large n with known sigma
applications.precision_reporting: Research papers report SE alongside point estimate
applications.power_analysis: Required n for target precision: n = (sigma * z / margin)^2

validation.sample_size_positive: n >= 2 (n = 1 gives undefined or zero SE)
validation.std_dev_non_negative: Both sigma and s must be >= 0
validation.at_least_one_std_dev: Either population_std_dev or sample_std_dev must be provided

missing_std_dev.description: Neither population nor sample std dev provided

errors.message: Sample size must be at least 2
Either population or sample standard deviation is required

anti_patterns.rule: Do not report standard deviation when standard error is required
anti_patterns.why: Journalists and analysts frequently conflate the two; SE is the correct uncertainty measure for an ESTIMATE, sigma is for individual OBSERVATIONS

anti_patterns.rule: Do not compute SE of the mean for autocorrelated data as if observations were independent
anti_patterns.why: Positive autocorrelation inflates true SE relative to the i.i.d. formula; HAC / Newey-West corrections or block bootstrap are required

anti_patterns.rule: Do not omit the finite population correction for large sampling fractions
anti_patterns.why: When n/N > 0.05, SE = (sigma/sqrt(n)) * sqrt((N-n)/(N-1)) is tighter; ignoring FPC overstates uncertainty for census-like samples

anti_patterns.rule: Do not use SE based on s when n is small (< 30) without switching to t-distribution
anti_patterns.why: Estimating sigma from s adds uncertainty that the normal distribution ignores; use t_(n-1) for small-sample inference

worked_example.interpretation: Sample mean return has estimated SE of 2.5 percentage points
